title: In-game gun-sight colour is turquoise/cyan
claim: WSSITE draws the site with colour command VGCTRQ*100+VGCOPC (VGCTRQ = colour code 3, turquoise). Our crosshair strokes with GLOW = '#00e5ff' (cockpit cyan). Turquoise ≈ cyan — the aiming reticle colour matches.
reasoning: VWCURS (WSSITE.MAC:48-50) loads `VGCTRQ*100+VGCOPC` as the SITE COLOR before drawing the cursor; VGCTRQ is the turquoise register. drawCrosshair (render.ts:698-740) strokes the cross + chevrons with GLOW, defined as '#00e5ff'. Both are the same cyan/turquoise family, so the reticle colour is faithful.

ours.verbatim: const GLOW = '#00e5ff' // cockpit cyan

title: Site travel is asymmetric (±5.5 grids H, ±4 grids V); ours clamps a symmetric ±1 NDC box
claim: WSSITE's VWPLAN limits the site/hood shift to ±5.5 grids horizontally (LDB #55.*2) and ±4 grids vertically (WSSITE.MAC:84, LDB #40.*2) — an asymmetric ~1.375:1 box that roughly tracks the 4:3 screen. crosshairNdc clamps aimX and aimY identically to [-1, 1] — a symmetric NDC square.
reasoning: The ROM's horizontal cursor range (5.5 grids) is larger than the vertical (4 grids); the ratio 5.5/4 = 1.375 approximates the cabinet's aspect. Our aim lives in normalised device coords and is clamped to the same ±1 on both axes (gameRules.ts:47), then mapped to the aspect-corrected viewport at draw time — so the on-screen reach ends up aspect-shaped too, but the underlying clamp is symmetric, not the ROM's authored ±5.5/±4. This is a coordinate-system difference (float NDC vs integer grids), not a gameplay bug.

source.verbatim: LDB #55.*2		;ALLOW +-5.5 GRIDS MOVEMENT, *2 COMPENSATES FOR MUL

ours.verbatim: return [clamp(aimX, -1, 1), clamp(aimY, -1, 1)]

title: No X-wing cockpit hood / four gun ports that parallax with the yoke
claim: VWPLAN (WSSITE.MAC:59-113) draws the visible X-wing cockpit: the hood bottom-middle (VJSTBM) and four laser gun ports — top-left (VJSTTR), bottom-left (VJSTBL), top-right (VJSTTL), bottom-right (VJSTBR) — all shifted by the yoke (SI.RSX/SI.RSY) to give an in-cockpit parallax. Our render draws no cockpit frame at all; the player laser flashes from fixed screen corners only at the instant of firing.
reasoning: The ROM composites a static-ish cockpit hood + gun barrels around the moving site so the player feels seated inside the X-wing; the barrels shift opposite the aim. render.ts has no cockpit-overlay pass — only the reticle (drawCrosshair) and the momentary converging laser lines from the four screen corners (render.ts:495-509). This is a deliberate simplification (no cockpit chrome), so there is no counterpart line to quote.

title: Trench banner text "EXHAUST PORT AHEAD" matches message EXH
claim: TCMES message EXH is <EXHAUST PORT AHEAD>. Our trench banner draws the identical string 'EXHAUST PORT AHEAD'.
reasoning: The message table (TCMES.MAC:610) defines EXH as the literal `EXHAUST PORT AHEAD`, shown as the port nears. drawTrenchBanners (render.ts:864) draws that exact string while the port is within range. Byte-for-byte text match.

ours.verbatim: glowText(ctx, 'EXHAUST PORT AHEAD', w / 2, h * 0.22, BANNER_TEXT_PX, 'center', '#dddddd', 14)

title: Banner text "DEATH STAR DESTROYED" matches message DSD
claim: TCMES message DSD is <DEATH STAR DESTROYED>. Our winning-shot banner draws the identical string 'DEATH STAR DESTROYED'.
reasoning: TCMES.MAC:611 defines DSD as `DEATH STAR DESTROYED`; render.ts:882 draws that exact string on the port kill. Text matches exactly.

ours.verbatim: glowText(ctx, 'DEATH STAR DESTROYED', w / 2, h * 0.45, BANNER_TEXT_PX, 'center', '#ffffff', 18)

title: Banner text "EXHAUST PORT MISSED" matches message MSD
claim: TCMES message MSD is <EXHAUST PORT MISSED>. Our failed-run banner draws the identical string 'EXHAUST PORT MISSED'.
reasoning: TCMES.MAC:612 defines MSD as `EXHAUST PORT MISSED`; render.ts:890 draws that exact string when the port slips past un-destroyed. Text matches exactly.

ours.verbatim: glowText(ctx, 'EXHAUST PORT MISSED', w / 2, h * 0.45, BANNER_TEXT_PX, 'center', '#ff5555', 16)

title: Game-over banner "GAME OVER" matches message GVR
claim: TCMES message GVR is <GAME OVER>. Our game-over screen draws the identical string 'GAME OVER'.
reasoning: TCMES.MAC:542 defines GVR as `GAME OVER` (colour TRQ). drawGameOver (render.ts:1008) draws that exact string. Text matches.

ours.verbatim: glowText(ctx, 'GAME OVER', w / 2, h * 0.24, BANNER_TEXT_PX, 'center', TIE_GLOW, 24)

title: Attract title "STAR WARS" matches the ROM marquee
claim: The ROM renders the marquee as the letters ST/A/R/W/A/RS (VWBNNR, TCMES.MAC:353-364) and also carries the message CPY = <STAR WARS>. Our attract screen title draws 'STAR WARS'.
reasoning: The banner phase spells S-T-A-R W-A-R-S with the VJSW* letter glyphs, and message CPY (TCMES.MAC:537) is the literal `STAR WARS`. drawAttract (render.ts:992) draws that exact string. The title text is faithful (our single static title vs the ROM's zooming banner is a separate motion nuance).

ours.verbatim: glowText(ctx, 'STAR WARS', w / 2, h * 0.26, TITLE_TEXT_PX, 'center', GLOW, 28)

title: "FOR USING THE FORCE" bonus banner text matches (amount is per-wave in ROM)
claim: The clean-trench-run reward is displayed as <n> followed by message < FOR USING THE FORCE> (TCMES.MAC:622, drawn by VWFRC in WSGAS which prints a 6-digit score then DOMESS on MS.USE+1). Our banner draws `${amount} FOR USING THE FORCE`. The trailing text matches exactly.
reasoning: VWFRC (WSGAS.MAC:419-437) views a per-wave force score (GETFRP → TSCFRC table) and then appends message MS.USE+1 = ` FOR USING THE FORCE`. Our render.ts:873 draws FORCE_BONUS (a FIXED 5,000) + ' FOR USING THE FORCE'. The string is confirmed; the prefixed AMOUNT differs — the ROM value scales with the wave, ours is fixed — but the exact point value is pair-score-shields' scope, not HUD's. Recording the text match here.

ours.verbatim: glowText(ctx, `${FORCE_BONUS.toLocaleString('en-US')} FOR USING THE FORCE`, w / 2, h * 0.16, BANNER_TEXT_PX, 'center', '#dddddd', 12)

title: Start prompt reads "PRESS START", not the ROM's "PULL TRIGGER TO START"
claim: The cabinet's start prompt is message STR = <PULL TRIGGER TO START> (TCMES.MAC:549). Our attract/game-over screens draw 'PRESS START'.
reasoning: The ROM has no 'PRESS START' string; the start invitation is `PULL TRIGGER TO START` (STR), consistent with the yoke-trigger cabinet. render.ts:993 (and 1020) draws 'PRESS START'. A text-content divergence — swap the string. (Our browser control is a key/click, so 'PULL TRIGGER' is aspirational, but the authentic wording is the ROM's.)

ours.verbatim: glowText(ctx, 'PRESS START', w / 2, h * 0.38, HUD_TEXT_PX, 'center', BOLT_GLOW, 12)

title: High-score board titled "HIGH SCORES"; the ROM titles it "PRINCESS LEIA'S REBEL FORCE"
claim: The hi-score table header is message RF2 = <PRINCESS LEIA'S REBEL FORCE> (RF1 = <REBEL FORCE> in the half-screen entry layout), MESSON'd by VWHSCR (TCHSCR.MAC:56-70). There is no 'HIGH SCORES' string in the ROM message table. Our board header draws 'HIGH SCORES'.
reasoning: VWHSCR posts MS.RF1/MS.RF2 above the table; the on-screen title is `PRINCESS LEIA'S REBEL FORCE` (TCMES.MAC:605). render.ts:1034 draws the generic 'HIGH SCORES'. Text-content divergence.

ours.verbatim: glowText(ctx, 'HIGH SCORES', w / 2, h * 0.5, HUD_TEXT_PX, 'center', GLOW, 10)

refutation_corrections: Two sub-claim inaccuracies in the finding, corrected from primary source: (1) 'RF1 = <REBEL FORCE>' is not supported — there is NO literal <REBEL FORCE> message in the table. TCMES.MAC:604 is `.NEXTMESS -320.,000.,VJMFL,RF1`, and .NEXTMESS (macro def TCMES.MAC:512-525) writes XY+color for a message SLOT but emits NO .ASCIN text of its own — it re-positions the FOLLOWING message's text. So RF1 is the SAME 'PRINCESS LEIA'S REBEL FORCE' header re-centred for the half-screen initials-entry layout (VWHSCR posts MS.RF1 'REBEL FORCE MIDDLE OF SCREEN' + THSY1 when UPDFLG is entering initials, TCHSCR.MAC:59-63), not a distinct short string. The comments' 'REBEL FORCE' is shorthand. (2) 'There is no HIGH SCORES string in the ROM message table' is imprecise: there IS `<RESET HIGH SCORES>` at TCMES.MAC:657 — but it is an OPERATOR SERVICE-MENU option (adjacent to RESET TIMING INFO / RESET OPTIONS / TEST NOVRAM / STARTING SHIELD), never a player-facing board title. The finding's conclusion is right for the player-facing title; the absolute 'no HIGH SCORES string' overstates it.

title: Initials-entry prompt reads "ENTER YOUR INITIALS", not "SHOOT YOUR INITIALS"
claim: The cabinet prompt for the initials screen is message HSZ = <SHOOT YOUR INITIALS> (TCMES.MAC:603), part of the HS1..HSZ block (`MESSAGE FROM REBEL COMMAND POST` / `YOU ARE A TRUE REBEL PILOT` / `THE FORCE IS WITH YOU` / `SHOOT YOUR INITIALS`). Our screen draws 'ENTER YOUR INITIALS'.
reasoning: The verb is SHOOT (you aim the site at a letter grid and fire — see H-013), not ENTER. render.ts:1015 draws 'ENTER YOUR INITIALS'. The wording divergence follows from the mechanism divergence; the string alone is an easy fix.

ours.verbatim: glowText(ctx, 'ENTER YOUR INITIALS', w / 2, h * 0.42, HUD_TEXT_PX, 'center', BOLT_GLOW, 12)

ruling: sw7-3: kept ENTER YOUR INITIALS. The ROM verb SHOOT assumes the shoot-a-letter-grid entry (H-013, accepted as structural); our clone uses keyboard entry, so ENTER accurately describes the input. Accepted divergence, not a fidelity miss.

title: Initials entry is shoot-a-letter-grid (flashing current initial); ours is keyboard typing
claim: GETINT (TCHSCR.MAC:523-610) enters initials by moving the site over an A-Z + RUB + END grid (TINTXY, octagon hit-test) and reading the FALLING EDGE of the fire button to commit each of 3 initials; the current initial FLASHES via FRAME+1 AND 01 (TCHSCR.MAC:107-109). Our entry reads keyboard letters/Backspace (stepNameEntry), with a static '_' cursor.
reasoning: The arcade flow: aim, hold on a letter, pull the trigger; RUB backs up, END commits; the in-progress letter blinks at ~frame/2 (≈10 Hz). main.ts:109-111 binds A-Z + Backspace to enterInitial (sim.ts:424-427 → @arcade/shared/name-entry), confirmed by a fresh Start press. This is a genuine input-model difference (yoke+trigger vs keyboard) inherent to a browser clone with no cabinet yoke — structural, not a bug. The flashing-current-initial affordance is also absent (render.ts:1017 draws a steady buffer + '_').

source.verbatim: LDA GN.SWE		;FALLING EDGE OF FIRE BUTTON ( SEE IFRAME IN MAIN )

ours.verbatim: if (/^[a-zA-Z]$/.test(e.key) || e.key === 'Backspace') state = enterInitial(state, e.key)

title: High-score table holds 10 entries (NHSCRS)
claim: The ROM keeps NHSCRS entries: UPDATE walks HSCORS until HSCORS+<4*NHSCRS> (TCHSCR.MAC:434) and DOINTS/INTSCR seeds exactly 10 default scores (TCHSCR.MAC:729-738). Our board caps at MAX_HIGH_SCORES = 10 (@arcade/shared/highscore), wired via makeHighScoreStorage.
reasoning: INTSCR lists 10 default words-pairs and INTINT 10 initials-triples, and NHSCRS bounds the scan/shift loops; the shared highscore module caps insertHighScore/qualifiesForHighScore at 10. Capacity matches. (Ours-side citation is the star-wars usage site; the numeric constant lives in @arcade/shared/highscore, which per the audit rule may not be cited from node_modules — noted as a limitation.)

ours.verbatim: const highScoreStorage = makeHighScoreStorage('star-wars', makeHighScoreRowGuard('wave'))

title: No seeded default high-score table (ROM ships OBI 1,285,353 … RLM 380,655)
claim: DOINTS copies 10 default entries from INTINT/INTSCR into the table on reset: initials OBI/WAN/HAN/GJR/MLH/JED/NLA/EJD/EAR/RLM (TCHSCR.MAC:718-727) with BCD scores 1,285,353 / 1,110,936 / 1,024,650 / 872,551 / 813,553 / 704,899 / 518,000 / 492,159 / 384,766 / 380,655 (TCHSCR.MAC:729-738). Our board starts empty and shows 'NO SCORES YET'.
reasoning: INTSCR:.WORD 0128,5353 decodes as the 8-digit BCD 01285353 = 1,285,353 (TCHSCR is RADIX 16, but each byte is a BCD digit-pair); INTINT:.BYTE 0F,02,09 = O(15),B(2),I(9) = OBI (A=1..Z=26). A fresh cabinet greets the player with those iconic Rebel names. render.ts:1037-1039 shows an empty board until a real score lands. Small: seed a default table.

ours.verbatim: if (highScores.length === 0) {

title: Three initials per high-score entry
claim: The ROM collects 3 initials (UPDINT cycles 0,1,2; CMPA #03 = all initials selected, TCHSCR.MAC:600). Our entry caps at MAX_INITIALS = 3.
reasoning: GETINT advances UPDINT and, at #03, sets INITL to END (TCHSCR.MAC:599-603); INITLS is 3 bytes per entry. sim.ts:404 defines MAX_INITIALS = 3, passed to stepNameEntry. The 3-character convention matches.

ours.verbatim: const MAX_INITIALS = 3

title: No rotating attract sequence — flight-instructions (INS) and scoring (SCR) pages are absent
claim: The attract loop cycles four phases: BNR (banner) → INS (flight instructions) → SCR (scoring page) → HIS (hi-score table) (WSMAIN TPHASE, WSMAIN.MAC:335-338). INS is a 5-rule briefing (FLI..FLZ, TCMES.MAC:553-568) and SCR is a per-object points page (SCR..SCZ: TIE 1,000 / Darth 2,000 / bunkers 200 / towers 200 / turrets 100 / fireballs 33 / exhaust port 25,000 / all tower tops 50,000, TCMES.MAC:573-581). Our attract screen is a single static STAR WARS title + start prompt + hi-score board — neither the instructions nor the scoring page exists.
reasoning: drawAttract (render.ts:986-998) renders only title + 'PRESS START' + board; there is no phase cycle and no INS/SCR content anywhere in the shell. Two whole attract pages missing. Sizeable (multi-page text layout + a rotation timer).

title: No intro story crawl (the receding "OBI-WAN KENOBI IS GONE …" special messages)
claim: The banner phase runs a set of 8 SPECIAL messages that recede into the distance (the "STAR WARS effect", VWSPMS/SPMESS): OBI-WAN KENOBI IS GONE BUT HIS / PRESENCE IS FELT WITHIN THE FORCE. / THE EMPIRE'S DEATH STAR, UNDER THE / COMMAND OF DARTH VADER, NEARS THE / REBEL PLANET.  YOU MUST JOIN THE / REBELLION TO STOP THE EMPIRE. / THE FORCE WILL BE WITH YOU. / ALWAYS (TCMES.MAC:625-632). Our clone has no story crawl.
reasoning: SPMESS entries are staged on an alarm table (TSPMAL) and scaled toward a vanishing point (VWSPMS, TCMES.MAC:245-286) — the iconic scrolling intro. Nothing in the shell renders these lines. Medium: a receding-text pass over the attract screen plus the eight strings.

title: In-run score uses comma grouping + leading-zero suppression, like VW8DIG
claim: The ROM score readout inserts a comma glyph (VJNUMS) every 3 digits and suppresses leading zeros via BT.SUP (VW8DIG/VWBYTE, TCMES.MAC:786-835). Our formatScore uses toLocaleString('en-US') → comma-grouped, no leading zeros.
reasoning: VWBYTE keeps BT.SUP counting down leading zeros to blanks and VW8DIG drops a VJNUMS comma after the high digit-pairs (TCMES.MAC:791). hud.ts:23-25 returns `points.toLocaleString('en-US')` (e.g. 12066 → '12,066'), matching the cabinet's comma grouping and zero-suppression for the in-run HUD score (render.ts:806).

ours.verbatim: return Math.max(0, Math.floor(points)).toLocaleString('en-US')

title: Game-over and high-score-board scores are NOT comma-grouped
claim: The ROM comma-groups every score it prints (VW8DIG inserts VJNUMS commas — TCMES.MAC:791 — and the hi-score table is drawn with VW8DIG, TCHSCR.MAC:239). Our game-over line draws the raw `SCORE ${state.score}` (render.ts:1009) and the board draws `String(e.score).padStart(6, ' ')` (render.ts:1044) — both without commas, unlike the comma-grouped in-run HUD (see H-019).
reasoning: formatScore (comma-grouped) is used for the in-run HUD but bypassed on the framing screens: the game-over score and the hi-score ladder print the bare integer. The ROM is consistent — VW8DIG everywhere — so a 6-digit board score shows '128,535' on a cabinet but '128535' here. Route these two sites through formatScore.

ours.verbatim: const pts = String(e.score).padStart(6, ' ')

title: No "50,000 FOR SHOOTING ALL TOWERS" reward banner (RWD)
claim: Clearing every surface tower shows message RWD = <50,000 FOR SHOOTING ALL TOWERS> (TCMES.MAC:609), alongside ATP = <CLEARED ALL LASER TOWERS>. We award the 50,000 (SURFACE_CLEAR_BONUS) and emit a tower-bonus event, but draw no banner — main.ts even flags it as a TODO.
reasoning: The all-towers bonus is banked in sim.ts and cued for audio (main.ts:191-197), whose own comment says "A bespoke '50,000 FOR SHOOTING ALL TOWERS' banner is a HUD follow-on." No HUD string is drawn. The reward text (and the paired 'CLEARED ALL LASER TOWERS') is missing. Small: one banner keyed off the existing tower-bonus stamp.

title: In-flight coaching / bonus HUD messages are absent (SFB/STF/ACW/BON/BRE/BSE/SHIELD GONE …)
claim: The ROM posts a family of in-play HUD messages our clone never shows: SHOOT FIREBALLS (SFB, TCMES.MAC:618), SHOOT TIE FIGHTERS (STF), AVOID CATWALKS (ACW), POINTS NEXT TOWER / TOWERS (NXT/TWR), STARTING WAVE BONUS (BON), BONUS FOR REMAINING ENERGY + ADDED TO DEFLECTOR SHIELD (BRE/BSE), and SHIELD GONE (SHG, drawn by VWGONE when energy hits zero). None of these appear in our HUD.
reasoning: render.ts draws only four trench banners plus the SCORE/WAVE/SHIELD header; the coaching prompts, the per-wave STARTING WAVE BONUS callout, the end-of-wave BONUS FOR REMAINING ENERGY tally, and the SHIELD GONE warning have no counterpart. Medium: a message-queue affordance plus ~8 strings and their triggers.

title: Shield gauge is a fuel-gauge (VJFUEL title + segmented bars); ours is a trapezoid+chevron+ticks with a "SHIELD" label
claim: VWGAS (WSGAS.MAC:113-163) draws the deflector energy as a titled gauge: the VJFUEL title graphic, bar segments selected from TGALL by S.GAS, and a single BCD numeral of the remaining count (VWBYT1). Our drawShieldMeter draws a wireframe trapezoid + centre chevron + per-shield tick marks + numeral + the literal word 'SHIELD'.
reasoning: The ROM's silhouette is a fuel/energy bar with a VJFUEL icon; the word 'SHIELD' appears in the ROM only as part of 'SHIELD GONE' (SHG). Our gauge (render.ts:921-963) is traced from a cabinet screenshot into a different vector shape and adds a 'SHIELD' text label (render.ts:961). Different rendering of the same datum (remaining shield energy) — structural, and the numeral itself does track the count.

ours.verbatim: function drawShieldMeter(ctx: CanvasRenderingContext2D, state: GameState, w: number): void {

title: Shield gauge colour is a fixed green; the ROM ramps colour by remaining energy (TGCLR)
claim: VWGAS indexes a colour table TGCLR by S.GAS*2 (WSGAS.MAC:121-124, repeated at 149-152) so the gauge changes colour as energy drops, and brightens on refuel (S.CAN → OR #0FF). Our gauge uses one constant HUD_SHIELD_COLOR = '#22e600' for every shield level.
reasoning: TGCLR gives a per-level colour ramp (healthy → warning → critical); our render.ts:787 hard-codes a single green and the comment already admits the low-shield ramp is unreproduced. This is a real divergence, but the exact TGCLR palette entries would need decoding from WSGAS's data tables before a faithful ramp could be authored — hence accept for now rather than a blind fix.

ours.verbatim: const HUD_SHIELD_COLOR = '#22e600' // PROVISIONAL(findings ## HUD & framing, ## Colors & intensities) — register pinned (healthy tier only), hex matched to a cabinet screenshot

refutation_corrections: Decoded TGCLR palette (index = S.GAS): 0 -> VGCOFF (blanked), 1-2 -> VGCRED (critical), 3-4 -> VGCYLW (warning), 5-9 -> VGCGRN (healthy); colours carry VGCOPC. Refuel pulse: while S.CAN>0, the selected colour is OR'd with #0FF (GSVALL, WSGAS.MAC:179-181) to flash bright, decrementing each frame. A faithful ramp is thus three fixed hues (green/yellow/red) keyed on the shield count plus a brief bright-flash on pickup — no interpolation.

title: Letterforms are a shared VGAN-lineage face; font comment mislabels provenance as "1981 ROM"
claim: Star Wars ships its own alphanumeric glyph table WSVGAN.MAC (RADIX 10; Ed Logg's VGAN 'UPDATED/REVISED/ENHANCED AND SUBJUGATED FOR STAR WARS', June '83 — WSVGAN.MAC:6), with stroke vectors per glyph plus special forms (½ via CH.HLF, © via CH.COP, tick-based comma/apostrophe). Our font module re-exports @arcade/shared/font and its comment calls it 'the 1981 ROM VGMSGA alphabet' — the shared cross-cabinet face (ADR-0002), not Star Wars' 1983 WSVGAN.
reasoning: Both descend from Atari's VGAN vector alphabet, so the caps-only forms are close, but our face is the shared library glyph set, not WSVGAN's byte-for-byte vectors, and the font.ts provenance note ('1981 ROM VGMSGA') is inaccurate for Star Wars (its VGMSGA is the 1983 WSVGAN adaptation). Deliberate shared-face convergence — structural — with a documentation nit worth correcting. The exact glyph geometry lives in @arcade/shared/font, which the audit rule forbids citing from node_modules (noted as a limitation).

source.verbatim: .SBTTL * UPDATED/REVISED/ENHANCED AND SUBJUGATED FOR STAR WARS

ours.verbatim: // The HUD / framing text is an authentic stroke-vector font (the 1981 ROM VGMSGA

title: Green predictive lock-on ring is non-ROM — the reticle is the bare site cursor, never a lock circle
claim: We rang the nearest TIE under the reticle with a green circle (render.ts drawLockOn, gameRules.ts LOCK_RADIUS_NDC=0.12, story 8-14) — a predictive 'your next shot connects' aim-assist. The 1983 site has no such ring: VWSITE ('VIEW ALL SITE STUFF') draws exactly the joystick-moved cursor (VWCURS) and the X-wing cockpit parts (VWPLAN, see H-003) — neither is a predictive lock circle. There is no lock ring anywhere in the 1983 source.
reasoning: No `lock`/`lock-on` exists in the 1983 source (grep hits are only inside BLOCK, in the DIVTST/MATEST/SND test files). VWSITE (WSSITE.MAC:37-40) is just `JSR VWCURS` (the joystick-moved cursor) + `JSR VWPLAN` (the X-wing cockpit hood/gun parts, H-003) — there is no ring routine among them. The cabinet's only target feedback is the site crosshair plus a POST-hit flash (LZ.HIT, the unported G-008) — feedback AFTER a hit, never a predictive ring before the shot lands. Object_12 (three concentric squares), once guessed to be a targeting/lock box, is `.WP PORT` — the THERMAL EXHAUST PORT (WSOBJ.MAC), SUPERSEDED in sw5-4. The QUANTITY the ring computed from IS authentic — the nearest object under the site, CLSLZ = min(CL.GDS, CL.ADS), the sw7-17 hitscan — but the cabinet never draws a ring around it. sw7-21 removed the ring for authenticity and kept the CLSLZ targeting (`beamHit`).

source.verbatim: VWSITE::			;VIEW ALL SITE STUFF WITH NORMAL COLORS

ours.verbatim: export const LOCK_RADIUS_NDC = 0.12
